blocks.content: The first phone call happened nine days in, a Thursday, eleven-thirty his time. She picked up on the second ring.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: Hey. Is this okay? It's late for you.
A beat. The sound of each other breathing across two thousand miles.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: Hard. The professor doesn't care if you're new. You're expected to show up with opinions.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: The opinions or the not caring part.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: Really good actually. My lit professor assigns things I've never read and then talks about them like they're living documents. It's — yeah. It's good.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: You were a little off the first few days. Texts were shorter.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: I know. I'm glad you are.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: Asleep. He goes to bed at ten every night. I respect it and resent it in equal measure.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: My roommate Claire keeps the string lights on until two AM but she's so nice I can't be annoyed about it.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: Don't tell Eli. He'll get some.

They stayed on the line another forty minutes talking about nothing in particular — his film history syllabus, a weird dining hall dish she'd tried, whether Eli had texted either of them yet (he had, extensively).
